asset_name: GHK-Cu Foundational Identity and Copper-Binding Evidence
status: FOUNDATIONAL PRIMARY IDENTITIES CONFIRMED — QUANTITATIVE EXTRACTION OPEN
domain: Isolation, sequence identity and copper-related biological function
extracted_summary: The foundational publication series identifies the plasma growth-modulating tripeptide as glycyl-histidyl-lysine.
A later primary publication proposed a copper-uptake function.
These records support identity and mechanism history, not cosmetic or injectable clinical efficacy.
limitations: Full primary articles were not directly extracted in Build 0019.
Historical biochemical observations do not establish modern product efficacy or quality.
permitted_claims: GHK is a naturally occurring glycyl-histidyl-lysine tripeptide with foundational plasma and copper-binding research.
prohibited_claims: Claims that endogenous occurrence proves injectable safety.
Claims that foundational identity research proves anti-ageing efficacy.
